analysisSummary: Confirmed by tracing internal/tui/cli/driver.go:328-348 and the disconnect path in consumeEvents. The driver treated large but valid stream-json lines as fatal scanner errors, which drops the active session when tool output crosses 1 MB.
suggestedFix: Raise the NDJSON scanner ceiling to match the larger tool-result payloads the TUI already handles elsewhere and lock the regression in with a >1 MB event test.

analysisSummary: Confirmed by tracing runSubprocess in internal/tui/mcp/spawner.go. The subprocess parser retained only the first 10 MB of NDJSON and then parsed that truncated buffer after exit, so the final result event could be dropped entirely for verbose agents.
suggestedFix: Capture the result entry as the NDJSON stream is scanned and fall back to that parsed result whenever the raw buffer truncates before post-run parsing.
